mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates for equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 0.3pp in 1 month

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 150k signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation above 285 points signals deteriorating real purchasing power that pressures corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 285

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and inflation expectations compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and real rate pressures, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between combined FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL signals and SPX 60d returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production above 104.5 points indicates expanding manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 35% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 104.5

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts below 1750 thousand units signals weak consumer demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST falls below 1750

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment below 3.6% indicates labor market tightness that drives wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE remains below 3.6%

mechanism_narrative: Combined declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand conditions that depress equity earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between combined RSAFS/HOUST signals and SPX 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window
